---
title: "AI for UVM Economics"
subtitle: "A practical bootcamp for faculty and graduate students"
---

## AI Bootcamp — Spring 2026

Two lunch sessions introducing AI tools for teaching and research. No prior experience required — just bring your laptop and curiosity.

### Schedule

| Session | Date | Focus | Slides |
|---------|------|-------|--------|
| Session 1 | **Wed, April 22** | Getting started: terminal, Claude Code, teaching applications | [HTML](slides/session1.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/session1.pdf) |
| Session 2 | **Mon, April 27** | Research pipelines, web scraping, automated grading | [HTML](slides/session2.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/session2.pdf) |

### Before you arrive

No installation needed — both sessions are watch-along demos. See the **[Prep Guide](prep.qmd)** if you want to try things on your own afterward.

### Presenters

- **Emily Beam** — Associate Professor of Economics, UVM
- **Erkmen Aslim** — Department of Economics, UVM

## About

This site hosts materials for the UVM Economics AI Bootcamp. After the bootcamp, it will grow into a broader resource for AI literacy in economics.

Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org). Source on [GitHub](https://github.com/eabeam/uvmecon-ai).
